thesis: 3D Systems is a legacy additive manufacturing pioneer executing a multi-year turnaround under CEO Jeffrey Graves. After years of revenue decline, Q1 2026 showed 11% YoY growth and positive adjusted EBITDA ($2.1M vs. -$23.9M prior year), driven by healthcare momentum and $55M in cost savings. The stock has rallied 45% in two weeks on turnaround optimism and is now overbought (RSI 75). The bull case hinges on sustained healthcare growth, industrial stabilization, and operating leverage from a leaner cost base. The bear case is that Q1 was a head-fake and the 3D printing winter continues. At <1x sales, the risk/reward is asymmetric — but only on a pullback.

financials.cash: Improved post-Geomagic sale; exact figure not disclosed in recent filings
financials.fcf: Negative historically; path to breakeven visible

drivers: Healthcare revenue sustains 20%+ annual growth with high margins
Industrial segment stabilizes and resumes modest growth
Cost savings program delivers permanent EBITDA margin expansion to 10%+
Strategic acquirer acquires DDD for IP and healthcare platform at a premium

risks: Q1 2026 results prove to be a head-fake; revenue resumes declining
Cost savings are exhausted and operating losses return
Dilutive capital raise required within 12 months
3D printing industry downturn persists, crushing hardware demand

base_case.assumptions: Revenue stabilizes around $400M annually with low-single-digit growth. Adjusted EBITDA remains slightly positive (2–5% margin) but does not inflect dramatically. Market values DDD at 1.0–1.2x sales, consistent with slow-growth industrial tech peers. No strategic buyer emerges.

catalysts.event: $55M cost savings program completion — margin impact visibility
Q2 2026 Earnings — critical test of EBITDA sustainability and revenue trend
Q3 2026 Earnings — full-year guidance update and 2027 outlook

key_risks: Turnaround execution risk — Q1 2026 may not be repeatable
Continued cash burn and potential dilutive capital raises
Intense competition from Stratasys, Desktop Metal, and Chinese AM vendors
Macro/industrial capex weakness crushes discretionary AM spending
Overbought technical condition (RSI 75) creates near-term pullback risk
